remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: PCOS is a lifelong endocrine disorder; complete spontaneous resolution rare; some phenotypic improvement with menopause; fertility returns in 50-70% with ovulation induction; metabolic features persist
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Weight loss of 5-10% restores ovulation in 55-90% of anovulatory PCOS women; metformin restores ovulation in 30-50%; letrozole (ovulation induction): 27% live birth rate per cycle (PPCOS II); lifestyle modification: 50-80% improvement in menstrual regularity
remission.gap_size: Large — lifestyle modification and metformin are evidence-based first-line but most patients receive OCPs (treating symptoms only, not the underlying metabolic disorder)
remission.barrier_detail: OCP (oral contraceptive pill) is most commonly prescribed for PCOS, treating symptoms (irregular periods, hirsutism) without addressing the underlying insulin resistance and metabolic risk; OCP does not treat infertility and may worsen insulin sensitivity; the PCOS field has lacked a clear pharmacotherapy standard
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — International PCOS Guideline updated; letrozole formally recommended over clomiphene for ovulation induction; metformin recommended as first-line metabolic treatment; GLP-1 agonists emerging as adjunct; spironolactone for hyperandrogenism; first guidelines were 2011 (Endocrine Society)
remission.outdated_treatments: Clomiphene citrate as first-line ovulation induction (inferior to letrozole — NEJM 2014 head-to-head); OCP as sole long-term treatment without metabolic assessment; ovarian drilling (laparoscopic — rarely indicated with letrozole available)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Inositol supplementation (myo-inositol + D-chiro-inositol) — multiple RCTs showing improvement in menstrual regularity, ovulation, insulin sensitivity comparable to metformin in some studies; dietary interventions (low-glycaemic diet — reduces insulin, androgens); N-acetylcysteine (NAC) as alternative to metformin in some cohorts; berberine
remission.frontier_signal: GLP-1 agonists (semaglutide significantly improves ovarian function, menstrual regularity, and fertility outcomes in PCOS — SPGP trial Phase 3); tirzepatide for PCOS metabolic phenotype; progesterone receptor modulators; gut microbiome modulation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
